theme: Close Every Loop — Every Prop, Every State, Every Interaction Should Speak

ideas.title: Error Count Stat Card — Red Flag in the Stats Bar
ideas.description: The StatsBar shows Total / Active / Idle / Sync / Vibe — but Errors, the most operationally urgent signal, have no dedicated card. The only way to see error count now is via the tab badge or by scanning all worker cards. Fix this with a 6th stat card rendered only when errorCount > 0, with a CSS entrance animation. Compute const errorCount = workers.filter(w => w.status === "error").length inside StatsBar. Style with red theming: border-color:rgba(239,68,68,0.45), background:rgba(239,68,68,0.06), value in bold red. Label: ❌ Errors. Entrance: @keyframes stat-error-in { from{transform:scale(0.7) translateY(10px);opacity:0} to{transform:none;opacity:1} }. When it drops back to 0, fade out via a 300ms exiting class then remove. WHY: Error count is a triage signal — it should be the most visible number on screen when nonzero. Burying it means engineers miss incidents. One stat card at the top of the dashboard changes the product from a pretty monitor to an actual ops tool.

ideas.title: Manual Sync Button + R Keyboard Shortcut — Respect the Power User
ideas.description: There is no way to force an immediate data refresh. The only Retry buttons appear on error banners which are rare. Power users babysitting deploys stare at stale data for 30 seconds with no recourse. Two-part fix: (1) Add a small <button className="sync-now-btn" onClick={syncFromGitHub} disabled={isSyncing}>↺</button> near the stats bar that spins when isSyncing. CSS: background:none; border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:6px; color:var(--text-muted). When disabled: animation:spin 0.7s linear infinite. @keyframes spin{to{transform:rotate(360deg)}}. (2) In the existing keyboard shortcut useEffect, add: else if ((e.key === "r" || e.key === "R") && !isSyncing) syncFromGitHub(). R for refresh is muscle memory from every monitoring tool ever built. WHY: A dashboard you trust is one you keep open. Trust comes from knowing you can poke it when you need to. The R shortcut costs 2 lines. The sync button costs 5 lines of CSS. Together they signal this tool was built by people who use it.

ideas.title: Vibe Change Cinematic Announcement — Your New Vibe Has Arrived
ideas.description: CARRY-FORWARD from review #11. The current vibe change: confetti fires if vibe improves, nothing happens if it worsens. Both directions deserve ceremony. Add a 2.5-second full-overlay announcement card. In App.jsx add const [vibeAnnouncement, setVibeAnnouncement] = useState(null). In the vibe streak useEffect, when prevKey !== null && prevKey !== teamVibe.key, call setVibeAnnouncement({ from: prevKey, to: teamVibe.key, label: teamVibe.label, emoji: teamVibe.emoji }). Then setTimeout(() => setVibeAnnouncement(null), 2500). Render as overlay with the vibe emoji large, the vibe name, and a "Vibe shift" label. CSS: position:fixed; inset:0; z-index:1000; pointer-events:none. Card animation: vibe-announce-in 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both, vibe-announce-out 0.4s ease 2s forwards. Suppress during initial load via the prevKey !== null guard. WHY: Vibe is the heartbeat of this product. When it shifts, it should feel like a moment — not a silent CSS class swap that users miss entirely.

ideas.title: Desk Monitor SOS Morse Code on Error — The Desk Is Screaming
ideas.description: CARRY-FORWARD from review #11. When a worker hits error status the desk gets a red tint but the .desk__monitor does nothing special. Error desks deserve a visceral signal: SOS in morse code flashing on the monitor. In Office.css, add .desk--error .desk__monitor { animation: sos-morse 4.2s step-start infinite; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--red), 0 0 12px rgba(239,68,68,0.4); }. Define sos-morse keyframes approximating the S-O-S dash-dot pattern: short-short-short (S), pause, long-long-long (O), pause, short-short-short (S) across 100% of the timeline using opacity toggling. CSS-only, zero JS. Add to the prefers-reduced-motion override block as animation:none. WHY: SOS is a universal distress signal. Engineers recognise the pattern subconsciously. A morse-code monitor creates ambient urgency without banners or alerts — just light, spelling help.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Completion Corkboard — The Wins Wall
ideas.description: CARRY-FORWARD from review #11. The dead wall space between the whiteboard and the manager desk is perfect for a tiny corkboard celebrating completions in real-time. Add .office-corkboard div in Office.jsx at left:21%, top:3%, 72px wide x 48px tall. Background: brown border with subtle crosshatch CSS pattern. Pass recentCompletions prop from App.jsx: activityLog.filter(e => e.type === "complete").slice(-3). Render up to 3 .corkboard-note children with pastel backgrounds (yellow/green/pink), each slightly rotated via --note-rot CSS variable, showing worker initial + task truncated to 12 chars. On note mount: animation: note-pin 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1). WHY: The office floor only reacts to failure (error glitches) and vibe. It never celebrates success. Three sticky notes that appear as the team ships is the difference between a monitoring dashboard and a team culture artifact.

ideas.title: Bloberto Stress Quotes on Active Errors — The Manager Notices
ideas.description: The speech bubble system uses VIBE_QUOTES with 4-5 lines per vibe — but errors are invisible to Bloberto. He blissfully quotes alignment strategy while the floor glitches red. Fix this by adding an ERROR_QUOTES array to Office.jsx: ["I see the red. I see it.", "We are treating this as a learning opportunity. Live.", "I am choosing not to look at the monitor right now.", "Everything is fine. I have defined fine very broadly.", "Have you tried turning the engineer off and on again?", "The SLA is... flexible. Very flexible."]. Accept errorCount prop on the Character component. In the ambient interval effect, check: if (errorCount > 0 && Math.random() < 0.6) { quotes = ERROR_QUOTES }. In Office.jsx render, pass errorCount={nonMgr.filter(w => w.status === "error").length} to the Bloberto Character. WHY: Cause and effect is the soul of a believable world. A manager who notices errors — nervously, in corporate doublespeak — makes the dashboard feel like a place where things happen, not a chart that refreshes every 30 seconds.

ideas.title: Idle Worker Thought Bubbles — The Floor Thinks
ideas.description: Idle workers wander the floor but say nothing. Working workers have typing dots. Ghosts have OOO bubbles. Idle is a storytelling void. Add random thought bubbles to idle wandering characters, firing once every 45-90s per worker, auto-dismissing after 3.5s. In the Character component for variant === "idle", add a useEffect with setInterval using a randomised delay. Define IDLE_THOUGHTS in Office.jsx: ["Should have pushed before lunch...", "Is this a good time to refactor the refactor?", "Coffee #3 imminent.", "I am not blocking anything. Technically.", "Just gonna check the news for a sec.", "Maybe if I stare at the backlog it will shrink.", "PR approved. I will merge it... later."]. Render as .thought-bubble (round cloud shape using border-radius, no triangle tail unlike speech-bubble). CSS: position:absolute; bottom:calc(100%+6px); background:rgba(20,18,50,0.88); border-radius:12px; padding:5px 9px; font-size:0.62rem; color:#c4b5fd; white-space:nowrap; pointer-events:none; z-index:150. WHY: Idle does not mean absent. Thought bubbles make the floor feel inhabited during slow periods — and this is the feature that makes people take screenshots and share them.

ideas.title: Animated Door on Worker Entry — Someone Just Walked In
ideas.description: When a new worker arrives, their Character plays the enter-office animation (slide up from bottom). But the door below just sits there unmoved. Fix this by tracking doorOpenActive state in Office.jsx. When workingWorkers.length increases vs a workingCountRef, set a 800ms doorOpenActive burst. Add door--open class to .office-door: .office-door--open .office-door__icon { animation: door-swing 0.7s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both; } @keyframes door-swing { 0%{transform:scale(1) rotate(0)} 35%{transform:scale(1.35) rotate(-20deg)} 70%{transform:scale(1.1) rotate(5deg)} 100%{transform:scale(1) rotate(0)} }. Also add a brief warm glow on the door frame during the swing. Track previous working count via useRef, compare on each render. WHY: The door is a stage prop waiting to be a story beat. The enter-office animation becomes 3x more meaningful when the door it came from swings open. Spatial continuity — someone actually walked through that door.

ideas.title: Coffee Corner Vibe Reaction — The Machine Knows
ideas.description: The coffee corner renders the same emoji at the same size regardless of whether the team is crushing it or half-asleep. Add data-vibe={vibe} to the .coffee-corner div in Office.jsx. CSS: .coffee-corner[data-vibe="crushing"] .coffee-corner__emoji { animation: coffee-surge 0.7s ease-in-out infinite; } @keyframes coffee-surge{0%,100%{transform:scale(1) rotate(-3deg)}50%{transform:scale(1.2) rotate(3deg)}}. .coffee-corner[data-vibe="slow-day"] .coffee-corner__emoji { opacity:0.55; filter:grayscale(0.4); }. .coffee-corner[data-vibe="after-hours"] .coffee-corner__label::after { content:" (decaf)"; font-size:0.38rem; color:var(--text-muted); }. Add a CSS steam pseudo-element above the cup that animates at speed proportional to vibe energy — fast during crushing, absent during slow-day. WHY: The coffee corner is a prop that could be a character. Vibe-reactive coffee is environment storytelling that rewards attentive viewers — the kind of hidden detail that becomes a feature people mention when they recommend this dashboard.

ideas.title: Confetti Rain on Vibe Drop — The Other Direction Also Has Ceremony
ideas.description: Confetti fires upward on vibe improvement — lovely. But a vibe drop (crushing to on-fire, in-flow to slow-day) produces zero visual response. The asymmetry is narratively strange: good news gets a party, bad news gets silence. Add a confetti rain effect for vibe drops. In the vibe streak useEffect, after the confetti burst condition, add: else if (prevKey !== null && vibeRanking[teamVibe.key] < (vibeRanking[prevKey] ?? 999)) { setConfettiRain(true); setTimeout(() => setConfettiRain(false), 2000); }. Add confettiRain state. Render alongside existing confetti layer: same pieces but with animation:confetti-fall instead of confetti-burst. @keyframes confetti-fall { from{transform:translateY(-40px) rotate(0deg);opacity:1} to{transform:translateY(calc(100vh + 20px)) rotate(720deg);opacity:0} }. Use darker, more muted piece colors (greys, dark purples) vs the bright celebration palette. WHY: Every good narrative has emotional range. Rain confetti is darkly funny — a visual joke about the team's fortunes reversing. It will make engineers laugh in the same moment it confirms bad news, which is exactly the tone this product lives in.
